thread.title: $4m in donations, Wicknell Chivayo’s astonishing 2024 spending spree
thread.title_full: $4m in donations, Wicknell Chivayo’s astonishing 2024 spending spree

author: Thando Kanye
title: $4m in donations, Wicknell Chivayo’s astonishing 2024 spending spree
text:
With over a month left until the end of 2024, it is estimated that controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has parted with almost $4million in a spending spree that has seen him turn into one of the country’s biggest philanthropists.
Chivayo, a one-time convict who was last year acquitted of corruption charges, has seemingly become the blue-eyed boy for Zimbabwean President Emmerson Mnangagwa, getting pictured with him several times and even traveling with him to official state functions.
Throughout that time, Wicknell has been thoroughly generous, making donations to people who he feels are deserving, particularly those aligned to the ruling party Zanu PF.
The latest beneficiary of his generosity is poverty stricken former Radio 2 hotshot Pashoma Ncube, who received a windfall of US$20 000 as well as a vehicle from the heavyset businessman.
According to H Metro, Wicknell has spent between $3million and $4million in donations this year alone.
Various Zimbabwean personalities, including well-known musicians, have been stampeding to get attention from Wicknell, who has been in a giving mood during the entirety of 2024.
Besides celebrities, Chivayo has also been generous towards those thought have links with Zanu-PF.
Jah Prayzah, who received a brand new S500 Mercedes-Benz worth US$180 000, Sulumami Chimbetu who received a brand-new 2022 Mercedes-Benz GLE 300 with an estimated market value of US$140 000, are just some of the musicians that have been spoilt by Wicknell.
Wicknell also gifted 50 50 Toyota Aqua vehicles to members of his Johanne Masowe Apostolic, before giving 20 more Aquas to the Scott Sakupwanya owned Scottland FC, for being crowned kings of the ZIFA Northern Region Division One championship.
The spending spree has raised eyebrows, as some wonder how a corruption accused businessman is splashing money unscathed in front of law and tax enforcement authorities.
Of course it helps when you move around with the President.
